description_long: This object is a vintage Commodore 1541 floppy disk drive, characterized by its rectangular, boxy shape and beige plastic exterior. The front panel features a horizontal slot for disk insertion, a prominent rectangular button, and indicator lights, all set within a black accent area. The rear panel is equipped with various ports, including a power switch, power socket, and data connectors, as well as ventilation grilles. The sides are plain, with subtle molding lines and no visible features. The overall design is utilitarian, with a matte finish and minimal decorative elements, typical of 1980s computer peripherals. Its size and weight suggest it is meant to rest on a flat surface, such as a desk or shelf.
description: A beige, rectangular Commodore 1541 floppy disk drive with a front disk slot, indicator lights, and rear connectivity ports. The design is utilitarian and typical of 1980s computer hardware.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the power switch, power socket, data connectors, and ventilation grilles.
description_view_1: The left side view is plain, showing only the smooth, beige plastic surface with subtle molding lines.
description_view_2: The front view displays the disk slot, indicator lights, and a rectangular button, all set within a black accent area.
description_view_3: The right side view is also plain, mirroring the left side with no visible features.
